description: Iraq invades Kuwait, removing a major OPEC producer from the market and triggering a sharp price spike on supply fears.
Currency collapses across Southeast Asia sharply reduce regional oil demand and pressure prices lower.
Attacks on the US trigger a short-term demand shock and broader market uncertainty.
US-led invasion disrupts Iraqi oil production and raises long-term supply risk premiums.
Lehman Brothers collapse triggers a global recession and the sharpest oil demand collapse in decades.
Unrest across the Middle East and North Africa halts Libyan oil exports and raises regional supply risk.

event_name: OPEC Maintains Production Despite Falling Prices
description: OPEC declines to cut output despite falling prices, accelerating a price collapse driven by the US shale boom.
Sanctions relief paves the way for Iranian oil to return to global markets, adding to oversupply concerns.
OPEC and non-OPEC producers (Russia included) agree to coordinated output cuts to support prices after the 2014-16 crash.
US withdraws from the JCPOA and reinstates sanctions, removing Iranian barrels from the market.
OPEC+ talks collapse and Saudi Arabia floods the market, crashing prices just as COVID-19 demand losses begin.
WHO declares a pandemic; global lockdowns cause the largest demand shock in oil market history, briefly sending US futures negative.
OPEC+ agrees to historic coordinated cuts of ~9.7 million bpd to stabilize the collapsed market.
Invasion triggers sanctions on Russian energy exports and a sharp spike in prices on supply disruption fears.

event_name: EU Ban and Price Cap on Russian Oil
description: EU embargo and G7 price cap on Russian seaborne crude reshape global trade flows and pricing.
